name: Ochsenschwanzsuppe (Ox Tail Soup)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 4-quart Dutch Oven, brown ox tails and onion in hot oil for several minutes.
2. Add water, salt, and peppercorns; simmer uncovered for about 2 hours.
3. Cover and continue to simmer for an additional 3 hours.
4. Add parsley, carrots, celery, bay leaf, tomatoes, and thyme; continue simmering for 30 more minutes or until vegetables are tender.
5. Strain the stock and refrigerate for at least one hour.
6. In a blender, puree the edible meat and vegetables. Reserve.
7. Remove fat from the top of the stock and reheat it.
8. In a large, dry frypan, brown flour over high heat until golden brown.
9. Cool slightly, then add butter or margarine, blending continuously.
10. Gradually add the stock and vegetables to the pan, stirring constantly.
11. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper as needed.
12. Add Madeira just before serving.
